title: Actuator: drive the coolant valve open on over-temp
body:
## What to build

When the control loop reads a temperature above the configured ceiling, it energizes the coolant-valve GPIO until the reading drops back under the hysteresis band. The decision logic is tested off-target against a fake HAL; the actual GPIO drive and valve movement are verified by a human on the bench.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Above the ceiling, the valve-open output is asserted (host test against a fake HAL)
- [ ] Below the hysteresis band, the output is de-asserted
- [ ] The decision logic is exercised entirely off-target in CI

## Bench Test Procedure

1. Flash the branch to the target and attach the coolant valve.
2. Heat the sensor past the ceiling; confirm the valve physically opens within 500 ms.
3. Let it cool; confirm the valve closes once under the hysteresis band.
4. Confirm no chatter around the threshold.

Parent: #41
Depends on: #42
